I agree with the initial conclusion that annealing is primarily recognized as a stress-relieving heat treatment. One additional point to consider is that this process softens the material by reducing dislocation density, which improves machinability—making it particularly valuable in manufacturing settings where cutting tools must work efficiently on tough metals.
